challenges.title: Simple Nav
challenges.description: Create a nav element containing an unordered list with at least 4 list items, each containing a link. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a nav with an unordered list of at least 4 links -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the
element' assert ' element' assert '' in html.lower(), 'Should close the
    element' assert html.lower().count('= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 list items' assert html.lower().count('= 4, 'Each list item should contain a link' assert html.lower().count('href=') >= 4, 'Each link should have an href attribute'
hints: Use href="#" as a placeholder for the link destinations

challenges.title: Accessible Nav
challenges.description: Create a nav element with an aria-label attribute. Inside, include an unordered list of at least 4 links. Mark one link as the current page using aria-current="page". Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create an accessible nav with aria-label -->
<!-- Mark one link as the current page with aria-current -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<nav' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <nav> element'
assert 'aria-label=' in html.lower(), 'Nav should have an aria-label attribute'
assert '<ul' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <ul> element'
assert html.lower().count('<li') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 list items'
assert html.lower().count('<a ') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 links'
assert 'aria-current="page"' in html.lower(), 'One link should have aria-current="page"'
hints: Add aria-label="Main navigation" to the <nav> element
Add aria-current="page" to the link representing the current page
These ARIA attributes help screen readers understand the navigation
